vba.sheets: ACCUEIL, ARTICLES, FOURNISSEURS, CONVENTIONS, MOUVEMENTS, TABLEAU_DE_BORD, ALERTE_DASHBOARD, INVENTAIRE, RAPPORTS, CALCULS_EOQ, HISTORIQUE, BON_RECEPTION, BON_SORTIE, BON_COMMANDE, DA_DEMANDE_ACHAT, GUIDE, VBA_MODULES, AUDIT_LOG, DASHBOARD, FORM_INPUT, BORDEREAU_COMMANDE, STAGING_BUFFER, SYS_STRINGS, RECEIPT_TAG, TEMPLATE_BON, System_Lists

backend.best-for: VBA code writing, French docs, XML analysis, daily driving

ai-backends.backend: Primary daily driver. Text-only. Reads: .bas, .frm, .cls, .txt, .xml, .json, .md. Cannot read images or PDFs. Best for token-optimized XML (handles structured context files well). 32K window fits full erp-project-context.xml + erp-admin-paths.xml.

backend.best-for: Code search, debugging, audit, quick explore

ai-backends.backend: Fast code analysis. Text-only. Reads: .bas, .frm, .cls, .txt, .xml, .json, .md. Faster than Llama for search-heavy tasks. Good for grepping XML context quickly.

backend.best-for: Large context reasoning, image analysis, PDF review, thesis

ai-backends.backend: Multimodal. Reads: .png, .jpg, .pdf, .bas, .frm, .cls, .txt, .xml, .json, .md. 1M token window can hold entire codebase + all XML context files + thesis. Good for: extracting design from PDF pages, reviewing thesis screenshots, analyzing VBA code across all modules at once. XML optimization is medium (Gemini processes XML but is less strict about structure than Groq models).

backend.best-for: Lightweight text tasks, fallback when Groq is overloaded

ai-backends.backend: Text-only. Reads: .bas, .frm, .cls, .txt, .xml, .json, .md. Smaller context but still fits all XML files. Good for routine VBA edits.

backend.best-for: Massive context tasks, full codebase review
backend.note: SKIPPED — SMS verification unavailable for NVIDIA NIM

ai-backends.backend: Requires NVIDIA NIM API key (SMS gate blocks registration). Fall back to Completions.me for large-context reasoning.

backend.note: Cerebras key: ACTIVE. Qwen3 235B via FreeLLM gateway.

ai-backends.backend: 235B MoE code model. Fast inference (~1s), no rate limits on Cerebras. Replaces OpenRouter qwen3-coder (was 429-prone via Venice).

backend.best-for: Auto-routed general inference via FreeLLM gateway
backend.note: 27 models across 11 providers. Keys active: Groq, Gemini, Cerebras, Cloudflare, GitHub Models.

ai-backends.backend: FreeLLM gateway on localhost:3000 aggregating all keyed providers. Meta-model 'free' picks first available. Auto-failover across providers.

backend.note: Direct provider in opencode.json. Key: ACTIVE. Unlimited requests.

ai-backends.backend: Completions.me — free unlimited access to Claude Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6, GPT-5.2. OpenAI-compatible API at completions.me/api/v1. Not routed through FreeLLM.

backend.best-for: Multimodal tasks, Gemma 4 26B via Gemini API
backend.note: Direct Google AI Studio. Replaces deprecated OpenRouter Venice route.

ai-backends.backend: Gemma 4 26B hosted on Google's API — free tier, ~2s response, 256K context.

backend.best-for: Offline VBA debugging, emergency edits without internet

ai-backends.backend: CPU-only text inference. Very slow (~30s). Reads: .bas, .frm, .cls, .txt, .xml, .json, .md (small files only). XML processing is weak — prefers plain text. Not suitable for images or large files.

backend.best-for: Last-resort offline fallback, simple VBA syntax review

ai-backends.backend: Smallest model. Very slow (~95s). Reads: tiny .bas files only. Cannot handle large XML context files. For extreme emergencies only.

backend.best-for: CPU reasoning tasks, when no internet available

ai-backends.backend: CPU reasoning model (1.4GB RAM). Good for logic/analysis under 16K ctx. Slower than cloud but usable offline. Reads: .bas, .txt, .md, .xml.

backend.best-for: CPU coding, best local model for VBA

ai-backends.backend: Best CPU coding model (2.5GB RAM). Strongest local option for VBA work. Still much slower than cloud (~25s/req). Reads: .bas, .txt, .md, .xml.

backend.note: Routes Anthropic Messages API → OpenRouter. Currently uses Nemotron 3 Super 120B for all tiers (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku). 1M context, free, text-only. Note: FreeLLM + Completions.me now provide better alternatives without proxy. Reads: .bas, .frm, .cls, .txt, .xml, .json, .md. Start: uv run uvicorn server:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8082 Use: OpenCode.bat fcc (auto-starts proxy) or OpenCode.bat proxy (server only).

backend.best-for: Complex multi-file VBA refactoring, PDF thesis review, image analysis
backend.note: Anthropic key in opencode.json (may be truncated). Needs billing.

ai-backends.backend: Best overall model. Multimodal. Reads: .png, .jpg, .pdf, .bas, .frm, .cls, .txt, .xml, .json, .md, .xlsx, .csv. 200K context handles entire VBA codebase + all XML context files + thesis. XML optimization is exceptional (best-in-class for structured data). Can read thesis PDFs, screenshots, and Excel exports directly. Recommended for complex tasks when credits are available.

backend.note: OpenAI key in opencode.json. Needs billing.

ai-backends.backend: Strong multimodal model. Reads: .png, .jpg, .pdf, .bas, .frm, .cls, .txt, .xml, .json, .md. Good XML processing. Faster than Claude but slightly less precise for VBA code. Good fallback when Claude is unavailable.

desktop-launchers.launcher: OpenCode.bat nemotron → Nemotron 120B (1M ctx, OpenRouter free)
OpenCode.bat gemini → Google Gemini 2.5 Flash (1M ctx)
OpenCode.bat gemma-local → Ollama Gemma 4 e2b (128K, offline)
OpenCode.bat ollama → Ollama server + interactive model picker
OpenCode.bat proxy → Start FCC proxy only (background)
OpenCode.bat autotest → AUTO macro test suite
OpenCode.bat automenu → AUTO mode selector menu

harness.commands: task-create, task-list, task-update, task-get, task-claim, bg-run, bg-check, worktree-create, worktree-remove, worktree-list, harness-compact

repo.note: Anthropic→OpenRouter proxy - limited utility (Anthropic endpoint needs credits)
